sub_criteria.name: Common Characteristics of Good Applications
sub_criteria.definition: The application demonstrates characteristics commonly found in strong grant applications.

signals.signal_text: Clear research vision and career trajectory
Distinctive ideas with an independent direction
Well-justified training plan
Strong mentorship and institutional support

sub_criteria.pros: The application describes a clear research vision regarding the care of PLWHIV and lists a team with relevant expertise (g.1.a, g.1.b). There is some evidence of institutional support through the involvement of GP academics and HIV specialists (g.1.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application lacks a specific 'training plan' for the applicant (g.1.c). The 'career trajectory' and 'independent direction' are implied through the project scope but not explicitly articulated as a personal career development narrative for the lead applicant (g.1.a, g.1.b). Mentorship is described in terms of team roles rather than a formal supervision framework for the applicant (g.1.d).
sub_criteria.rationale: The application lacks a specific 'training plan' for the applicant (g.1.c). The 'career trajectory' and 'independent direction' are implied through the project scope but not explicitly articulated as a personal career development narrative for the lead applicant (g.1.a, g.1.b). Mentorship is described in terms of team roles rather than a formal supervision framework for the applicant (g.1.d).

sub_criteria.name: Tell Us Why You Need This Award
sub_criteria.definition: The applicant clearly articulates why this specific award is the right next step in their career.
sub_criteria.group_name: Tell Us Why You Need This Award

signals.signal_text: Explain why this award is the right next step in your career (not just another project grant)
What difference would securing an award make to your career, and why now?
How will an award build on your existing experience and what new skills will it enable you to gain?
How will it develop your research partnerships within the NHS, public health or social care, charity organisations, industry or other organisations?

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided text.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text focuses entirely on the research project details and does not contain a section or statement explaining why this specific award is the right next step for the applicant's career, the difference it would make, or how it builds on existing experience (g.2.a, g.2.b, g.2.c). There is no explicit discussion of how the award will develop research partnerships beyond the project team itself (g.2.d).
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text focuses entirely on the research project details and does not contain a section or statement explaining why this specific award is the right next step for the applicant's career, the difference it would make, or how it builds on existing experience (g.2.a, g.2.b, g.2.c). There is no explicit discussion of how the award will develop research partnerships beyond the project team itself (g.2.d).

sub_criteria.name: Relevant Research Experience and Training
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrates prior research experience and/or training that prepares the applicant to successfully undertake the proposed award in applied health or social care research.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of previous projects, research methods, or techniques relevant to the proposed study
Evidence of formal training, courses, or certifications
Alignment between prior experience and skills required for the proposed project

sub_criteria.pros: The 'Research Team' section explicitly details prior experience with NIHR grants, electronic health records analysis, and scoping reviews, which directly aligns with the proposed methods (g.3.a, g.3.c). Specific mentions of PhD research and successful grant applications provide strong evidence of relevant experience.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit mention of formal training courses, certifications, or workshops completed by the applicant (g.3.b). The evidence is largely team-based rather than focused solely on the lead applicant's individual training history.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit mention of formal training courses, certifications, or workshops completed by the applicant (g.3.b). The evidence is largely team-based rather than focused solely on the lead applicant's individual training history.

sub_criteria.name: Impactful Research Outputs and Outcomes
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence of meaningful research outputs or outcomes appropriate to the applicant’s career stage, demonstrating contribution to the field.

signals.signal_text: Number and quality of publications, reports, or presentations
Citation metrics or other indicators of impact (where mentioned)
Awards, grants, or recognitions linked to prior research
Description of practical or societal impact of previous work

sub_criteria.pros: The text mentions a 'track record of publications' and 'successful NIHR grant applications' for key team members, indicating prior research outputs (g.4.a, g.4.c). The project aims to generate evidence for clinical guidance, implying an understanding of practical impact (g.4.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No specific citation metrics, impact factors, or quantitative indicators of impact are provided (g.4.b). The description of practical impact is prospective (what the project will do) rather than a detailed description of the impact of *previous* work (g.4.d). The evidence is somewhat generic ('track record') rather than listing specific high-impact outputs.
sub_criteria.rationale: No specific citation metrics, impact factors, or quantitative indicators of impact are provided (g.4.b). The description of practical impact is prospective (what the project will do) rather than a detailed description of the impact of *previous* work (g.4.d). The evidence is somewhat generic ('track record') rather than listing specific high-impact outputs.

sub_criteria.name: Trajectory Towards Research Leadership
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrates potential and progression towards becoming a future leader in health or social care research.

signals.signal_text: Statements of career ambitions and long-term goals
Evidence of increasing responsibility in previous roles (e.g., leading projects, mentoring)
Participation in leadership or professional development programs
Recognition by peers or institutions suggesting leadership potential

sub_criteria.pros: The applicant is listed as a 'Joint lead applicant' and has led previous PhD research, suggesting some increasing responsibility (g.5.b). Membership in committees (e.g., BHIVA external relations) suggests some peer recognition (g.5.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There are no explicit statements of long-term career ambitions or goals (g.5.a). No participation in specific leadership or professional development programs is mentioned (g.5.c). The evidence for leadership potential is inferred from current roles rather than a demonstrated trajectory of increasing responsibility over time.
sub_criteria.rationale: There are no explicit statements of long-term career ambitions or goals (g.5.a). No participation in specific leadership or professional development programs is mentioned (g.5.c). The evidence for leadership potential is inferred from current roles rather than a demonstrated trajectory of increasing responsibility over time.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Practitioner-Academic Career (if applicable)
sub_criteria.definition: For applicants including a clinical or practice element, shows a clear commitment to combining research with professional practice.

signals.signal_text: Explicit statement of practitioner-academic career intentions
Involvement in clinical or practice-based research
Evidence of balancing practice responsibilities with research activities
Participation in relevant professional networks or qualifications

sub_criteria.pros: The team includes 'GP academics' and 'HIV specialists', demonstrating strong involvement in clinical and practice-based research (g.6.b). The applicant's role in analyzing electronic health records and focus groups for a PhD funded by NIHR indicates relevant professional network participation (g.6.d).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is no explicit statement of the applicant's intention to pursue a practitioner-academic career path (g.6.a). The text does not describe how the applicant balances clinical practice responsibilities with research activities (g.6.c).
sub_criteria.rationale: There is no explicit statement of the applicant's intention to pursue a practitioner-academic career path (g.6.a). The text does not describe how the applicant balances clinical practice responsibilities with research activities (g.6.c).

sub_criteria.name: Quality of the Plain English Summary
sub_criteria.definition: Clear, simple description of the research that a non-specialist audience can understand; explains problem, importance, plan, beneficiaries, and real-world use; avoids jargon; aligns with the detailed proposal.

signals.signal_text: Readability scores (e.g., Flesch-Kincaid)
Low density of unexplained jargon or technical terms
Presence of problem statement, objectives, methods, beneficiaries, and real-world impact
Alignment with detailed proposal content
Sentence structure and coherence for non-specialist understanding
Emphasis on societal or patient relevance

sub_criteria.pros: The Plain English Summary is highly readable (Flesch-Kincaid grade 5.3, Reading Ease 74.35) with very low jargon density (2.41%). It clearly articulates the problem (ageing PLWHIV, lack of GP guidance), objectives (develop care model), methods (review, database analysis, co-design), beneficiaries (PLWHIV, GPs), and impact (longer, healthier lives). The text aligns perfectly with the Detailed Research Plan, using accessible language throughout.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: None identified; the summary meets all criteria for clarity, simplicity, and alignment.
sub_criteria.rationale: None identified; the summary meets all criteria for clarity, simplicity, and alignment.

sub_criteria.name: Evidence Review Quality & Relevance
sub_criteria.definition: How comprehensively and critically the application reviews existing literature and evidence.

signals.signal_text: Number and relevance of references cited
Presence of critical analysis vs. mere summary
Identification of knowledge gaps
Coherence and logical flow

sub_criteria.pros: The application provides a robust review of existing evidence, citing specific reports (BHIVA 2017) and systematic reviews. It critically analyzes three care models (standard, shared, collaborative), noting limitations in evidence quality and consistency. It clearly identifies knowledge gaps regarding current GP practices and the need for an up-to-date scoping review.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the review is strong, the detailed plan relies heavily on a single scoping review (BHIVA 2017) as the primary baseline, though it acknowledges the need to update this. The critical analysis is strong but could be slightly more expansive on recent grey literature beyond the cited reviews.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the review is strong, the detailed plan relies heavily on a single scoping review (BHIVA 2017) as the primary baseline, though it acknowledges the need to update this. The critical analysis is strong but could be slightly more expansive on recent grey literature beyond the cited reviews.

sub_criteria.name: Research Scope & Award Appropriateness
sub_criteria.definition: Whether the project scope matches the scale and expectations of the proposed award (doctoral, postdoctoral, etc.).

signals.signal_text: Alignment of aims with award level
Feasibility indicators (time, complexity, tasks)
Proportion of work relative to award expectations

sub_criteria.pros: The scope is well-defined with three distinct workstreams (scoping review, database analysis, co-design) and clear timelines (months 2-16). The 18-month duration and £180k budget align well with the complexity of the tasks. The project is feasible, utilizing existing databases (CPRD) and established frameworks (PBA).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No significant drawbacks; the scope is appropriately matched to the award level and resources.
sub_criteria.rationale: No significant drawbacks; the scope is appropriately matched to the award level and resources.

sub_criteria.name: Research Design & Methodological Rigor
sub_criteria.definition: Clarity, appropriateness, and robustness of study design to answer the research questions.

signals.signal_text: Explicit description of methods
Logical alignment between aims and methods
Discussion of controls, sample size, or methodology justification
Structured methodological sections

sub_criteria.pros: Methods are explicitly described: Arksey and O'Malley framework for the scoping review, CPRD Aurum database for quantitative analysis, and PBA for co-design. There is strong alignment between aims and methods. Sample size justification is provided (acknowledging HIV rarity), and statistical analysis (regression) is detailed.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: Sample size justification notes that a minimum cannot be prescribed due to the rarity of HIV and potential under-reporting in GP records, which is a limitation but is acknowledged and managed through the study design.
sub_criteria.rationale: Sample size justification notes that a minimum cannot be prescribed due to the rarity of HIV and potential under-reporting in GP records, which is a limitation but is acknowledged and managed through the study design.

sub_criteria.name: Potential Impact on Patients, Public, and Health Services
sub_criteria.definition: Likely benefits or influence of the research on patients, carers, or health and care systems.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of direct or indirect patient/public benefits
Alignment with health priorities or societal needs
Clarity and specificity of expected outcomes

sub_criteria.pros: The application clearly states benefits for PLWHIV (longer, healthier lives) and the NHS (effective resource use). It aligns with NHS priorities (Five Year Forward View, Long Term Plan). Expected outcomes are specific: a co-designed model of care ready for piloting and eventual adoption in clinical guidelines.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: None identified; impact is clearly articulated and directly linked to patient and system benefits.
sub_criteria.rationale: None identified; impact is clearly articulated and directly linked to patient and system benefits.

sub_criteria.name: Engagement with People & Communities
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence of meaningful involvement of patients, carers, or communities.

signals.signal_text: Use of co-design, participatory methods, or consultations
References to ethical considerations and feedback mechanisms
Description of involvement at multiple project stages

sub_criteria.pros: Strong evidence of engagement using the Person-Based Approach (PBA). PLWHIV are co-applicants and co-lead the steering group. Engagement spans all stages: proposal writing, study design, focus groups, and dissemination. Ethical considerations and compensation for participants are explicitly mentioned.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: None identified; engagement is deep, meaningful, and integrated throughout the project lifecycle.
sub_criteria.rationale: None identified; engagement is deep, meaningful, and integrated throughout the project lifecycle.

sub_criteria.name: Justification of Resources & Award Costs
sub_criteria.definition: Appropriateness and clarity of requested resources for the planned work.

signals.signal_text: Explicit links between tasks and resources requested
Budget proportionality to project scope
Clear rationale for funding level

sub_criteria.pros: The total cost (£180,401.32) and duration (18 months) are clearly stated. The application mentions that compensation for stakeholder time and expenses is costed in the budget. The scope of work (three workstreams) appears proportional to the funding level.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text does not provide a detailed breakdown of the budget (e.g., specific line items for staff, travel, or equipment) to fully justify the links between specific tasks and resources requested. The justification is inferred from the scope rather than explicitly detailed in a budget table.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text does not provide a detailed breakdown of the budget (e.g., specific line items for staff, travel, or equipment) to fully justify the links between specific tasks and resources requested. The justification is inferred from the scope rather than explicitly detailed in a budget table.

sub_criteria.name: Quality of Previous Research (if applicable)
sub_criteria.definition: Prior relevant research experience demonstrating capability to deliver the proposed project.

signals.signal_text: Number and relevance of publications or outputs
Evidence of prior successful projects
Skills alignment with proposed work

sub_criteria.pros: The research team demonstrates strong prior experience: successful NIHR grant applications, publications on HIV care, and expertise in electronic health records and scoping reviews. Skills are well-aligned with the proposed work (e.g., RM's PhD on EHRs, SD's PBA expertise).
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the team's track record is strong, the application does not list specific publication titles or citation counts for the lead applicant individually, relying more on general statements of 'track record' and 'successful applications'.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the team's track record is strong, the application does not list specific publication titles or citation counts for the lead applicant individually, relying more on general statements of 'track record' and 'successful applications'.

sub_criteria.name: Inclusive Research Design
sub_criteria.definition: Consideration of inclusivity throughout the research lifecycle, including participant diversity and accessibility.

signals.signal_text: Explicit strategies for diverse recruitment
Adaptive methods or accessibility considerations
Equity and participation indicators

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly addresses diversity, noting that PLWHIV are not homogenous and highlighting specific groups (MSM, Black African, trans). It plans to recruit underserved demographics for focus groups and ensure the stakeholder group reflects these demographics. Equity is a stated consideration in the analysis of care access.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While strategies for diverse recruitment are mentioned, specific adaptive methods for accessibility (e.g., for disabled participants) are not detailed beyond general statements of 'extra efforts' and 'online options'.
sub_criteria.rationale: While strategies for diverse recruitment are mentioned, specific adaptive methods for accessibility (e.g., for disabled participants) are not detailed beyond general statements of 'extra efforts' and 'online options'.

sub_criteria.name: Quality and Relevance of Proposed Training
sub_criteria.definition: How well-structured, clear, and relevant the training plan is in relation to the project’s goals and the applicant’s research needs.

signals.signal_text: Completeness and clarity of training description
Alignment of training topics with project methods or techniques
Structured timeline or sequencing of training activities
Explicit links between training content and skill acquisition

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided text regarding a training plan.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text provided is limited to summary information and does not contain a training section. There is no description of training activities, timelines, or links to skill acquisition.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text provided is limited to summary information and does not contain a training section. There is no description of training activities, timelines, or links to skill acquisition.

sub_criteria.name: Suitability to Personal Development and Project Needs
sub_criteria.definition: How well the proposed training addresses the applicant’s current skills gaps and supports successful project delivery.

signals.signal_text: Identification of personal skill gaps
Clear mapping between training activities and project tasks
Evidence that proposed training is targeted rather than generic
Mention of mentorship, workshops, or formal courses relevant to development needs

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided text regarding personal development needs or training suitability.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The provided text lacks any mention of the applicant's skill gaps, specific training needs, or how training would support project delivery. The belief state explicitly lists td.2 as missing.
sub_criteria.rationale: The provided text lacks any mention of the applicant's skill gaps, specific training needs, or how training would support project delivery. The belief state explicitly lists td.2 as missing.

sub_criteria.name: Support for Career Aspirations
sub_criteria.definition: The extent to which the training plan, and any professional practice components, contribute to the applicant’s long-term career goals.

signals.signal_text: Explicit statement of career goals
Connections between training activities and future career trajectory
Indications of skill transferability beyond the current project
Reference to professional practice or experiential learning where applicable

sub_criteria.pros: No evidence found in the provided text regarding career aspirations or training support for them.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The application text does not contain statements about career goals, connections between training and future trajectory, or professional practice components. The belief state explicitly lists td.3 as missing.
sub_criteria.rationale: The application text does not contain statements about career goals, connections between training and future trajectory, or professional practice components. The belief state explicitly lists td.3 as missing.

sub_criteria.name: Track Record of the Contracting Organisation (and Partners)
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstrated expertise, experience, and research achievements of the organisation(s) in the proposed research area.

signals.signal_text: Publication or project history in relevant research fields
Previous funding success or awards in similar domains
Mention of prior collaborations or partnerships
Highlighted achievements or notable outputs

sub_criteria.pros: The application provides strong evidence of the team's track record, explicitly mentioning 'successful NIHR grant applications' and 'track record of publications' for key members (JH, WL, JVR) in the relevant field of HIV care and general practice. It also highlights specific prior outputs like a PhD funded by NIHR and published work on PBA and scoping reviews.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the team's individual track records are well-documented, the evidence focuses primarily on the researchers rather than the contracting organisation (Integrated Care Board) or the host institution's broader research achievements and infrastructure. The link between the specific organisation's history and the project is less explicit than the team's history.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the team's individual track records are well-documented, the evidence focuses primarily on the researchers rather than the contracting organisation (Integrated Care Board) or the host institution's broader research achievements and infrastructure. The link between the specific organisation's history and the project is less explicit than the team's history.

sub_criteria.name: Suitability of Supervisory Support and Mentorship Arrangements
sub_criteria.definition: Quality, clarity, and appropriateness of supervision and mentoring for supporting the applicant’s research and career development.

signals.signal_text: Explicit description of supervisor roles and responsibilities
Evidence of mentorship plans, meetings, or training support
Alignment between supervisor expertise and project needs
Presence of structured supervision framework (frequency, format, accountability)

sub_criteria.pros: The application clearly defines specific roles for supervision and mentorship, such as MTR providing 'methodological and statistical advice' and 'help supervise the senior research associate,' and SD providing 'expert advice' on PBA. The alignment between supervisor expertise (e.g., GP academics, HIV specialists, PBA experts) and project needs is excellent. A stakeholder group is established to oversee workstreams, providing a structured support mechanism.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While roles are defined, the specific frequency, format, and accountability mechanisms for the formal supervision of the lead applicant or the research team (e.g., 'monthly meetings') are not explicitly detailed in the text, relying more on the description of the stakeholder group's quarterly meetings.
sub_criteria.rationale: While roles are defined, the specific frequency, format, and accountability mechanisms for the formal supervision of the lead applicant or the research team (e.g., 'monthly meetings') are not explicitly detailed in the text, relying more on the description of the stakeholder group's quarterly meetings.

sub_criteria.name: Appropriateness of the Contracting Organisation to Support the Award and Career Aspirations
sub_criteria.definition: How well the organisation (and any partners) can provide the infrastructure, resources, and environment necessary to deliver the project and support the applicant’s career growth.

signals.signal_text: Description of facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided
Links between organisational support and project success
Alignment of organisational focus with applicant’s career trajectory
Evidence of tailored support for award type (doctoral, postdoctoral)

sub_criteria.pros: The application identifies the contracting organisation as an 'INTEGRATED CARE BOARD' and notes that GP academics on the team will provide 'access to networks of primary care clinicians for study recruitment,' indicating some organisational support for access. The team's mix of clinical and research expertise suggests an environment capable of supporting the project.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: There is very limited specific evidence regarding the facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided by the contracting organisation itself. The text does not describe the organisation's specific alignment with the applicant's career trajectory or tailored support mechanisms for the award type (e.g., specific training facilities, administrative support structures). The evidence is largely inferred from the team's capabilities rather than the organisation's explicit offerings.
sub_criteria.rationale: There is very limited specific evidence regarding the facilities, infrastructure, or resources provided by the contracting organisation itself. The text does not describe the organisation's specific alignment with the applicant's career trajectory or tailored support mechanisms for the award type (e.g., specific training facilities, administrative support structures). The evidence is largely inferred from the team's capabilities rather than the organisation's explicit offerings.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Inclusive and Supportive Research Culture
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence that the organisation fosters inclusivity, research integrity, and a supportive environment for all researchers.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of policies or practices promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion
Evidence of inclusive recruitment, mentoring, or support initiatives
References to adherence to research integrity principles
Statements on organisational culture, training, or initiatives supporting underrepresented groups

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates a strong commitment to inclusivity through the research design, explicitly stating that the stakeholder group will reflect demographics most affected by HIV (MSM, Black African, trans) and those underrepresented. It mentions 'Equality, diversity and inclusion' as a specific section and plans to recruit from underserved groups. The involvement of PLWHIV as co-applicants and in the stakeholder group shows a supportive culture for lived experience.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The evidence for an inclusive culture is primarily project-specific (how the team will behave) rather than evidence of the contracting organisation's broader policies, institutional initiatives, or adherence to research integrity principles. There is no mention of the organisation's specific diversity policies or training programs for staff.
sub_criteria.rationale: The evidence for an inclusive culture is primarily project-specific (how the team will behave) rather than evidence of the contracting organisation's broader policies, institutional initiatives, or adherence to research integrity principles. There is no mention of the organisation's specific diversity policies or training programs for staff.

sub_criteria.name: Integration of Lived Experience in Research Design
sub_criteria.definition: How input from patients, carers, or people with relevant lived experience has shaped the research questions, design, and methods.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of consultation or co-design activities
Specific examples of changes made to study design based on lived experience
References to patient or community advisory groups

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly details the use of the Person-Based Approach (PBA) with PLWHIV as co-applicants and steering group co-leaders. It describes specific co-design activities (focus groups, steering group meetings) where lived experience directly shapes the intervention model and guiding principles.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the plan for co-design is robust, the application describes the *process* of making changes based on feedback rather than providing specific examples of *past* changes made to the study design prior to submission (though the iterative nature is clear).
sub_criteria.rationale: While the plan for co-design is robust, the application describes the *process* of making changes based on feedback rather than providing specific examples of *past* changes made to the study design prior to submission (though the iterative nature is clear).

sub_criteria.name: Diversity and Inclusivity of the Research Team
sub_criteria.definition: Extent to which the research team reflects diversity in terms of skills, experience, and demographic representation.

signals.signal_text: Explicit mentions of team composition across gender, ethnicity, age, or other relevant characteristics
Statements about inclusion in hiring or team-building
Evidence of equitable task allocation and collaboration

sub_criteria.pros: The team composition is explicitly diverse, including two PLWHIV as co-applicants, GP academics, HIV specialists, and policy researchers. Roles are clearly allocated, with PLWHIV leading PPI/stakeholder engagement and co-leading the steering group.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the team includes diverse professional backgrounds and lived experience, there is no explicit statement regarding the demographic diversity (e.g., gender, ethnicity, age) of the academic/research staff themselves, only the participants they aim to recruit.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the team includes diverse professional backgrounds and lived experience, there is no explicit statement regarding the demographic diversity (e.g., gender, ethnicity, age) of the academic/research staff themselves, only the participants they aim to recruit.

sub_criteria.name: Commitment to Maximising Public and Patient Benefit
sub_criteria.definition: Clarity and strength of the plan to ensure research outcomes meaningfully benefit patients, carers, and the public.

signals.signal_text: Mentions of dissemination, knowledge translation, or implementation plans
Clear links between study objectives and anticipated benefits
Emphasis on actionable outcomes or improvements in health/care

sub_criteria.pros: A comprehensive dissemination strategy is outlined, targeting professionals, policymakers, and the public via journals, conferences, social media, and press releases. The link between the study objectives (optimizing care) and anticipated benefits (longer, healthier lives, clinical guidance) is explicit.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the plan mentions sharing findings with PLWHIV via peer networks, the specific mechanisms for ensuring the *actionable* outcomes are directly accessible and usable by the patient community (beyond general news stories) could be slightly more detailed.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the plan mentions sharing findings with PLWHIV via peer networks, the specific mechanisms for ensuring the *actionable* outcomes are directly accessible and usable by the patient community (beyond general news stories) could be slightly more detailed.

sub_criteria.name: Representation of Seldom Heard or Underserved Groups
sub_criteria.definition: Degree to which participants and PPI contributors represent groups historically underrepresented in research, including those facing health inequalities.

signals.signal_text: Mention of specific demographic groups (age, race, ethnicity, disability, gender, sexual orientation, religion/belief, location, socioeconomic status)
Evidence of targeted recruitment strategies
Discussion of barriers to participation and mitigation strategies

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly identifies specific underserved groups (MSM, Black African, trans/gender diverse, non-metropolitan residents) and acknowledges barriers like stigma and racism. It details targeted recruitment strategies, including face-to-face meetings in different locations and online options.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While barriers are identified and strategies mentioned, the specific mitigation tactics for overcoming deep-seated stigma or ensuring the safety of trans participants in focus groups are not elaborated in granular detail.
sub_criteria.rationale: While barriers are identified and strategies mentioned, the specific mitigation tactics for overcoming deep-seated stigma or ensuring the safety of trans participants in focus groups are not elaborated in granular detail.

sub_criteria.name: Inclusivity Across the Research Lifecycle
sub_criteria.definition: Demonstration that inclusion is considered at all stages of the project: planning, recruitment, conduct, analysis, and dissemination.

signals.signal_text: References to inclusion in study design, participant selection, and analysis
Consideration of accessibility and equity in all project stages
Documented plans for ongoing evaluation and adaptation

sub_criteria.pros: Inclusion is embedded across the lifecycle: PBA in design, diverse recruitment in focus groups, stakeholder feedback in analysis/refinement, and inclusive dissemination. The plan explicitly considers accessibility (online options, reimbursement) and equity.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The plan for ongoing evaluation and adaptation of inclusion strategies during the project execution is implied through the iterative PBA process but not explicitly described as a standalone monitoring activity.
sub_criteria.rationale: The plan for ongoing evaluation and adaptation of inclusion strategies during the project execution is implied through the iterative PBA process but not explicitly described as a standalone monitoring activity.

sub_criteria.name: Costed and Resourced Plan for Research Inclusion
sub_criteria.definition: Evidence that research inclusion has been properly budgeted and resourced within the proposal.

signals.signal_text: Explicit budget line(s) for inclusion activities (PPI, outreach, recruitment support)
Mention of staff time, materials, or training dedicated to inclusion
Alignment between inclusion plan and allocated resources

sub_criteria.pros: The application explicitly states that compensation for time and expenses for the stakeholder group is costed in the budget. It also mentions training on PBA for the group and reimbursement for focus group participants.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the text confirms that costs are included in the budget, it does not provide a specific budget line item breakdown or the exact monetary amounts allocated for these inclusion activities, which limits the ability to verify the adequacy of the resources.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the text confirms that costs are included in the budget, it does not provide a specific budget line item breakdown or the exact monetary amounts allocated for these inclusion activities, which limits the ability to verify the adequacy of the resources.

sub_criteria.name: Use of Formatting, Headings, and Subheadings
sub_criteria.definition: Effective use of text formatting to make the application clear, navigable, and easy to read.

signals.signal_text: Presence and consistency of headings and subheadings
Use of bold or emphasis for key terms
Structured hierarchy of sections (logical nesting of headings)
Patterns detected via text parsing for formatting markers

sub_criteria.pros: The application form analysis confirms the presence of 10 numbered heading-like lines and 22 bullet/numbered list markers, indicating strong use of headings and subheadings. A structured hierarchy is evident with distinct sections (Plain English Summary, Detailed Research Plan, etc.) and logical nesting. Text parsing detected 13 emphasis markers, supporting the use of formatting for key terms.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: The analysis notes a parser limitation where bold/emphasis markers may be lost during text extraction; while 13 markers were detected, the full extent of emphasis usage cannot be perfectly verified, preventing a perfect score for af.1.b.
sub_criteria.rationale: The analysis notes a parser limitation where bold/emphasis markers may be lost during text extraction; while 13 markers were detected, the full extent of emphasis usage cannot be perfectly verified, preventing a perfect score for af.1.b.

sub_criteria.name: Limited Duplication and Repetition
sub_criteria.definition: Avoidance of unnecessary repetition within or across sections.

signals.signal_text: Semantic similarity measures between sentences/paragraphs
Detection of repeated phrases or ideas
Novelty score for each section (unique content vs. overlap)

sub_criteria.pros: The analysis explicitly reports a duplicate sentence rate of 0.0 and a repeated line rate of 0.0. No high cross-section lexical overlap was detected at the 0.18 threshold, indicating strong novelty and lack of unnecessary repetition across sections.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: No drawbacks identified; the metrics for duplication and repetition are optimal.
sub_criteria.rationale: No drawbacks identified; the metrics for duplication and repetition are optimal.

sub_criteria.name: Logical Flow and Coherence Across Sections
sub_criteria.definition: The application progresses logically, with a clear thread linking the research question, objectives, and work packages.

signals.signal_text: Semantic coherence between sections (using embeddings or similarity metrics)
Explicit linking of objectives to methods and work packages
Presence of transitional phrases or linking statements
Detectable narrative thread anchored on research aims

sub_criteria.pros: The application demonstrates logical flow with 7 transition phrases and 42 objective-method-budget link terms. The section order follows a standard logical progression from applicant context to research plan, PPI, training, and budget, supporting a coherent narrative thread.
sub_criteria.drawbacks: While the structural indicators (transition phrases, link terms) are strong, the analysis relies on keyword counts rather than deep semantic embedding analysis to confirm the narrative thread, leaving a minor gap in verifying the depth of semantic coherence.
sub_criteria.rationale: While the structural indicators (transition phrases, link terms) are strong, the analysis relies on keyword counts rather than deep semantic embedding analysis to confirm the narrative thread, leaving a minor gap in verifying the depth of semantic coherence.
